title: Thank You to Our Members!
summary: October marked the first month of KRBD moving forward without federal funding. We are now 100% powered by people and local businesses who support us as members and underwriters. On October 11 & 12th, KRBD was challenged to raise $50,000 to meet a challenge issued by a very generous anonymous donor....

title: Governor declares disaster as storm floods west and north Alaska communities
summary: Governor Mike Dunleavy declared the 2025 October West Coast Storm a disaster this afternoon, after flooding hit communities along Alaska’s west and north coasts.

title: Updated unofficial election results show sales tax cuts hold strong, candidate leads widening
summary: Only one position has changed, while the rest remain consistent with first round results

title: AST Assault: Akiak [AK25103229]
summary: On October 10th, 2025, the Alaska State Troopers received a report of an assault in Akiak. Victim and witness statements indicated that Edwin Nicolai, 46, of Akiak had assaulted a juvenile male. After the juvenile male left, he began assaulting his girlfriend, at which point the girlfriend's daughte...
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

entities.address_detail: which point Nicolai was located and placed under arrest

summary: On 10/11/2025 at approximately 0527 hours, AST received a report of a vehicle collision near mile 106 of the Parks Highway. The following investigation revealed that Alphonsus Pete Jr, 27 YOA, was driving while under the influence of alcohol. Alphonsus was arrested and transported to MSPT, where he....
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: AST Warrant Arrests: Badger [AK25098433]
summary: On 10/11/2025 at approximately 0500 hours Troopers conducted traffic stop on vehicle in Badger. Identified 4 individuals with active warrants. Devin Starnes, Sterling Gilbert, Joshua Allison and Brittany Blue arrested and remand to FCC.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

entities.address_detail: approximately 0500 hours Troopers conducted traffic stop on vehicle in Badger

title: AST Assault: North Pole [AK25103361]
summary: On 10/10/2025, at 2313 hours Troopers responded to an assault at a party spot on Brock Road in North Pole, the investigation is still on going. Troopers are requesting any information regarding this event. Case officer is Trooper Anderson, if you have information email Kyler.anderson@alaska.gov.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

entities.address_detail: a party spot on Brock Road in North Pole

title: AST False Information, Warrant Arrest: Palmer [AK25102998]
summary: On 10/10/25 at approximately 0456hrs AST conducted a traffic stop on a vehicle for failing to dim its bright lights to oncoming traffic on Bogard Rd. The driver and front passenger did not provide sufficient evidence of their identity and were subsequently taken into custody for the violations of fa...
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

entities.address_detail: approximately 0456hrs AST conducted a traffic stop on a vehicle for failing to dim its bright lights to oncoming traffic on Bogard Rd

title: Alaska education officials walk back proposal to restrict local governments’ funding for schools
summary: A school bus is seen parked outside of Xóots Elementary School in Sitka on Oct. 6, 2025 (Photo by Corinne Smith/Alaska Beacon) Officials with the Alaska Department of Education and Early Development have walked back a proposal to limit local governments’ funding for public schools, instead asking the State Board of Education to take no action on the item this week. The State Board of Education was set to vote this week on a controversial measure that many school officials and education advocates say would bar local municipalities from providing much-needed funding and support services to local schools. Instead the board voted unanimously on Thursday, at a DEED official’s request, to...

title: National Republican campaign group must comply with subpoena, Alaska Supreme Court says
summary: (Photo by Krisanapong Detraphiphat/Getty Images) A national group that backed Gov. Mike Dunleavy’s re-election campaign in 2022 must comply with a subpoena issued as part of a yearslong investigation , the Alaska Supreme Court ruled Friday. In a 13-page decision , the court rejected a series of argu...

title: Amid tight budget picture, $50 million investment draws scrutiny from legislators and governor
summary: Adam Crum, commissioner of the Alaska Department of Revenue, testifies Friday, Jan. 20, 2023, at a meeting of the Alaska Senate Finance Committee in the state Capitol at Juneau, Alaska. (Photo by James Brooks/Alaska Beacon) As Alaska lawmakers contemplate the need to spend from savings to balance next year’s state budget, a unique investment by the state’s departing revenue commissioner is drawing their attention. Shortly before leaving office in July to run for governor as a Republican candidate, former commissioner Adam Crum approved a $50 million private equity investment with the fi...

title: Alaska Supreme Court upholds dismissal of lawsuit challenging Yukon-Kuskokwim salmon management
summary: A Chinook salmon is seen in an undated photo. (Photo by Ryan Hagerty/USFWS) Alaska judges will not hear a lawsuit alleging that the Alaska Department of Fish and Game has mishandled the state’s valuable salmon returns. On Friday, the Alaska Supreme Court upheld a lower-court decision that dismissed...

title: Timber company re-applies to store logs in Lutak Inlet after surveying sea floor
summary: The public has until Oct. 13 to comment on the permit application. Fishing and subsistence interests are concerned. The post Timber company re-applies to store logs in Lutak Inlet after surveying sea floor appeared first on KHNS Radio | KHNS FM .

title: Documentary examining Skagway during the pandemic set to release at film festival
summary: For most, the pandemic was a worldwide nightmare, one of uncertainty, sickness, isolation and masks. However for Stan Bush, he saw a different story. “I think that our film, ‘Last Call in the North,’ opens and ends with the best parts of Skagway and life up in Southeast Alaska,” he said. “It’s a […]...
